commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a private bundled `snapshot` plugin scaffold with a public API barrel, a SQLite snapshot provider/local repository implementation, WAL-mode tests, labeler ownership, and lockfile updates.

PR surface: Source +393, Tests +110, Config +28, Other +18. Total +549 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings by source inspection: the PR head reads whole artifacts in `sha256File`, changes lockfile resolution, and adds a second `VACUUM INTO` snapshot implementation beside current backup code. The feature itself is not a broken existing behavior report.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected `maintainer` label, draft RFC dependency, and missing real contributor proof make the next step maintainer/product review rather than a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: Add snapshot provider proof plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a bundled `snapshot` plugin scaffold under `extensions/snapshot` without exposing the public CLI yet.
- Adds a reusable `SqliteSnapshotProvider` contract plus a local snapshot repository implementation.
- Creates SQLite snapshots with `VACUUM INTO`, writes `manifest.json` + `database.sqlite`, verifies SHA-256/size metadata, and runs SQLite `PRAGMA integrity_check`.
- Proves committed WAL-mode state is captured without copying live `-wal`/`-shm` sidecars.
- Adds labeler ownership for `extensions/snapshot`.

## Scope

This is PR 1 from the snapshot RFC roadmap: provider proof only. It intentionally does not add `openclaw snapshot ...` commands, backup integration, remote storage, leases, scheduling, or failover orchestration.

RFC context: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/20
Related storage discussion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90370

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/snapshot`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions\snapshot\index.ts extensions\snapshot\api.ts extensions\snapshot\src\snapshot-provider.ts extensions\snapshot\src\manifest.ts extensions\snapshot\src\sqlite-snapshot.ts extensions\snapshot\src\local-repository.ts extensions\snapshot\src\snapshot-provider.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: SQLite snapshot provider can create, verify, and internally restore a compact snapshot artifact from a WAL-mode SQLite database.
Real environment tested: Windows source checkout, Node/Vitest extension test runner against `node:sqlite`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Test creates a WAL-mode source database, commits a row after `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`, snapshots via the local provider, verifies the manifest and artifact, restores to a fresh path, and reads both pre-WAL and committed-in-WAL rows from the restored database.
Observed result after fix: 1 test file passed; 2 provider tests passed.
What was not tested: Public `openclaw snapshot` CLI, backup command integration, object storage providers, scheduled snapshots, leases, and fresh OpenClaw state-dir boot proof; those are intentionally later PRs.
target.author: giodl73-repo
